title: carya borealis
intro: Carya borealis, also known as the Black Walnut, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America, known for its distinctive black bark and edible nuts. The medicinal properties of Carya borealis lie in its hulls, which contain a compound called juglone, a natural fungicide and insecticide. The health benefits of consuming Carya borealis include its potential to combat fungal infections, parasites, and certain types of cancer, as well as its ability to support digestive health and immune function. Herbal preparations of Carya borealis can be made into capsules, tinctures, or teas, and are often used to support overall well-being and as a natural remedy for various health issues.
